verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus lifted up His eyes, and said, Father, I thank Thee that Thou hast heard Me; and I knew that Thou hearest Me always, but because of the people which stand by, I said it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What I have often said, I will now say, that Christ looketh not so much to His own honor as to our salvation; not how He may utter some sublime saying, but how something able to draw us to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account His sublime and mighty sayings are few, and those also hidden, but the humble and lowly are many, and abound through His discourses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since by these men were the rather brought over, in these He continueth; and He doth not on the one hand utter these universally, lest the men that should come after should receive damage, nor, on the other hand, doth He entirely withhold those, lest the men of that time should be offended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since they who have passed from lowmindedness unto perfection, will be able from even a single sublime doctrine to discern the whole, but those who were ever lowminded, unless they had often heard these lowly sayings, would not have come to Him at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In fact, even after so many such sayings they do not remain firm, but even stone and persecute Him, and try to kill Him, and call Him blasphemer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when He maketh Himself equal with God, they say, “This man blasphemeth” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); and when He saith, “Thy sins be forgiven thee” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 20 ), they moreover call Him a demoniac.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So when He saith that the man who heareth His words is stronger than death, or, “I am in the Father and the Father in Me” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 51 ), they leave Him; and again, they are offended when He saith that He came down from heaven. ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If now they could not bear these sayings, though seldom uttered, scarcely, had His discourse been always sublime, had it been of this texture, would they have given heed to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He saith, “As the Father commanded Me, so I speak” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 31 ); and, “I am not come of Myself” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 28 ), then they believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That they did believe then is clear, from the Evangelist signifying this besides, and saying, “As He spake these words, many believed on Him.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then lowly speaking drew men to faith, and high speaking scared them away, must it not be a mark of extreme folly not to see at a glance how to reckon the sole reason of those lowly sayings, namely, that they were uttered because of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since in another place when He had desired to say some high thing, He withheld it, adding this reason, and saying, “Lest we should offend them, cast a hook into the sea.” ( Matt. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which also He doth here; for after saying, “I know that Thou hearest Me always,” He addeth, “but because of the multitude which standeth around I said it, that they might believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Are these words ours?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is this a human conjecture?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then a man will not endure to be persuaded by what is written, that they were offended at sublime things, how, when he heareth Christ saying that He spake in a lowly manner that they might not be offended, how, after that, shall he suspect that the mean sayings belonged to His nature, not to His condescension?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So in another place, when a voice came down from heaven, He said, “This voice came not because of Me, but for your sakes.” ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who is exalted may be allowed to speak lowly things of himself, but it is not lawful for the humble to utter concerning himself anything grand or sublime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the former ariseth from condescension, and has for its cause the weakness of the hearers; or rather (it has for its cause) the leading them to humblemindedness, and His being clothed in flesh, and the teaching the hearers to say nothing great concerning themselves, and His being deemed an enemy of God, and not being believed to have come from God, His being suspected of breaking the Law, and the fact that the hearers looked on Him with an evil eye, and were ill disposed towards Him, because He said that He was equal to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But that a lowly man should say any great thing of Himself, hath no cause either reasonable or unreasonable; it can only be folly, impudence, and unpardonable boldness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth Christ speak humbly, being of that ineffable and great Substance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the reasons mentioned, and that He might not be deemed unbegotten; for Paul seems to have feared some such thing as this; wherefore he saith, “Except Him who did put all things under Him.” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This it is impious even to think of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if being less than Him who begat Him, and of a different Substance, He had been deemed equal, would He not have used every means that this might not be thought?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now He doth the contrary, saying, “If I do not the works of Him that sent Me, believe Me not.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed His saying, that “I am in the Father and the Father in Me” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ), intimateth to us the equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It would have behooved, if He had been inferior, to refute this opinion with much vehemence, and not at all to have said, “I am in the Father and the Father in me” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 30 ), or that, “We are One,” or that, “He that hath seen Me, hath seen the Father.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus also, when His discourse was concerning power, He said, “I and the Father are One”; and when His discourse was concerning authority, He said again, “For as the Father raiseth up the dead and quickeneth them, even so the Son quickeneth whom He wilt” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 21 ); which it would be impossible that He should do were He of a different substance; or even allowing that it were possible, yet it would not have behooved to say this, lest they should suspect that the substance was one and the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if in order that they may not suppose Him to be an enemy of God, He often even uttereth words unsuited to Him, much more should He then have done so; but now, His saying, “That they should honor the Son even as they honor the Father” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23 ); His saying, “The works which He doeth, I do also” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ); His saying that He is “the Resurrection, and the Life, and the Light of the world” ( c. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25; c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 ), are the expressions of One making Himself equal to Him who begat Him, and confirming the suspicion which they entertained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He maketh this speech and defense, to show that He broke not the Law, and that He not only doth not remove, but even confirmeth the opinion of His equality with the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also when they said, “Thou blasphemest, because thou makest thyself God” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33 ), from equality of works He established this thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why say I that the Son did this, when the Father also who took not the flesh doeth the same thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He also endured that many lowly things should be said concerning Him for the salvation of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the, “Adam, where art thou?” ( Gen. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ), and, “That I may know whether they have done altogether according to the cry of it” ( Gen. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 21 ); and, “Now I know that thou fearest God” ( Gen. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 ); and, “If they will hear” ( Ezek. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 ); and, “If they will understand” ( Deut. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ); and, “Who shall give the heart of this people to be so?” and the expression, “There is none like unto Thee among the gods, O Lord” ( Ps. lxxx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ); these and many other like sentences in the Old Testament, if a man should pick them out, he will find to be unworthy of the dignity of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the case of Ahab it is said, “Who shall entice Ahab for Me?” ( 2 Chron. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the continually preferring Himself to the gods of the heathen in the way of comparison, all these things are unworthy of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet in another way they are made worthy of Him, for He is so kind, that for our salvation He careth not for expressions which become His dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed, the becoming man is unworthy of Him, and the taking the form of a servant, and the speaking humble words, and the being clothed in humble (garments), unworthy if one looks to His dignity, but worthy if one consider the unspeakable riches of His lovingkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And there is another cause of the humility of His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is that they knew and confessed the Father, but Him they knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He continually betaketh Himself to the Father as being confessed by them, because He Himself was not as yet deemed worthy of credit; not on account of any inferiority of His own, but because of the folly and infirmity of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He prayeth, and saith, “Father, I thank Thee that Thou hast heard Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if He quickeneth whom He will, and quickeneth in like manner as doth the Father, wherefore doth He call upon Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it is time now to go through the passage from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then they took up the stone where the dead man lay.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus lifted up His eyes, and said, Father, I thank Thee that Thou hast heard Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I knew that Thou hearest Me always, but because of the people that stand by I said it, that they might believe that Thou hast sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then ask the heretic, Did He receive an impulse from the prayer, and so raise the dead man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did He work other miracles without prayer? saying, “Thou evil spirit, I charge thee, come out of him” ( Mark ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ); and, “I will, be thou clean” ( Mark i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 41 ); and, “Arise, take up thy bed” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8 ); and, “Thy sins be forgiven thee” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ); and to the sea, “Peace, be still.” ( Mark iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In short, what hath He more than the Apostles, if so be that He also worketh by prayer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather I should say, that neither did they work all with prayer, but often they wrought without prayer, calling upon the Name of Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now, if His Name had such great power, how could He have needed prayer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He needed prayer, His Name would not have availed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He wholly made man, what manner of prayer did He need? was there not then great equality of honor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let Us make,” It saith, “man.” ( Gen. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What could be greater sign of weakness, if He needed prayer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us see what the prayer was; “I thank Thee that Thou hast heard Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who now ever prayed in this manner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Before uttering any prayer, He saith, “I thank Thee,” showing that He needed not prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I knew that Thou hearest Me always.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said not as though He Himself were powerless, but to show that His will and the Father’s is one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did He assume the form of prayer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear, not me, but Himself, saying, “For the sake of the people which stand by, that they may believe that Thou hast sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, "That they may believe that I am inferior, that I have need of an impulse from above, that without prayer I cannot do anything; but, “That Thou hast sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all these things the prayer declareth, if we take it simply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Thou hast sent me weak, acknowledging servitude, and doing nothing of Myself”; but dismissing all these things, that thou mayest have no such suspicions, He putteth the real cause of the prayer, “That they may not deem Me an enemy of God; that they may not say, He is not of God, that I may show them that the work hath been done according to Thy will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All but saying, “Had I been an enemy of God, what is done would not have succeeded,” but the, “Thou heardest Me,” is said in the case of friends and equals.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I knew that Thou hearest Me always,” that is, “in order that My will be done I need no prayer, except to persuade men that to Thee and Me belongeth one will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then prayest Thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the sake of the weak and grosser sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And when He had thus spoken, He cried with a loud voice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why said He not, “In the name of My Father come forth”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or why said He not, “Father, raise him up”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did he omit all these expressions, and after assuming the attitude of one praying, show by His actions His independent authority?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because this also was a part of His wisdom, to show condescension by words, but by His deeds, power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they had nothing else to charge Him with except that He was not of God, and since in this way they deceived many, He on this account most abundantly proveth this very point by what He saith, and in the way that their infirmity required.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was in His power by other means to show at once His agreement with the Father and His own dignity, but the multitude could not ascend so far.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Lazarus, come forth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is that of which He spake, “The hour is coming, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and they that hear shall live.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, that thou mightest not think that He received the power of working from another, He taught thee this before, and gave proof by deeds, and said not, Arise, but, “Come forth,” conversing with the dead man as though living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What can be equal to this authority?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if He doth it not by His own strength, what shall He have more than the Apostles, who say, “Why look ye so earnestly on us as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?” ( Acts iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if, not working by His own power, He did not add what the Apostles said concerning themselves, they will in a manner be more truly wise than He, because they refused the glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in another place, “Why do ye these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We also are men of like passions as you.” ( Acts xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Apostles since they did nothing of themselves, spoke in this way to persuade men of this; but He when the like opinion was formed concerning Him, would He not have removed the suspicion, if at least He did not act by His own authority?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who would assert this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in truth Christ doeth the contrary, when He saith, “Because of the people which stand by I said it, that they might believe”; so that had they believed, there would have been no need of prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if prayer were not beneath His dignity, why should He account them the cause of His praying?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why said He not, “I do it in order that they may believe that I am not equal to Thee”; for He ought on account of the suspicion to have come to this point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He was suspected of breaking the Law, He used the very expression, even when they had not said anything, “Think not that I am come to destroy the Law” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ); but in this place He establisheth their suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In fact, what need was there at all of going such a round, and of using such dark sayings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It had been enough to say, “I am not equal,” and to be rid of the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But what,” saith some one, “did He not say that, I do not My own will?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even this He did in a covert way, and one suited to their infirmity, and from the same cause through which the prayer was made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what meaneth “That Thou hast heard Me”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It meaneth, “That there is nothing on My part opposed to Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then the, “That Thou hast heard Me,” is not the saying of one declaring, that of Himself He had not the power, (for were this the case, it would be not only impotence but ignorance, if before praying He did not know that God would grant the prayer; and if He knew not, how was it that He said, “I go that I may awake him,” instead of, “I go to pray My Father to awake him?”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then this expression is a sign, not of weakness, but of identity of will, so also is the, “Thou hearest Me always.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We must then either say this, or else that it was addressed to their suspicions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If now He was neither ignorant nor weak, it is clear that He uttereth these lowly words, that thou mayest be persuaded by their very excess, and mayest be compelled to confess, that they suit not His dignity, but are from condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say the enemies of truth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He spake not those words, Thou hast heard me,” saith some one, “to the infirmity of the hearers, but in order to show a superiority.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet this was not to show a superiority, but to humble Himself greatly, and to show Himself as having nothing more than man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to pray is not proper to God, nor to the sharer of the Throne.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou then that He came to this from no other cause than their unbelief?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe at least that the action beareth witness to His authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He called, and the dead man came forth wrapped.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then that the matter might not seem to be an appearance, (for his coming forth bound did not seem to be less marvelous than his resurrection,) Jesus commanded to loose him, in order that having touched and having been near him, they might see that it was really he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou His freedom from boastfulness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He doth not lead him on, nor bid him go about with Him, lest He should seem to any to be showing him; so well knew He how to observe moderation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When the sign had been wrought, some wondered, others went and told it to the Pharisees.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then did they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they ought to have been astonished and to have admired Him, they took counsel to kill Him who had raised the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They thought to give up to death Him who had overcome death in the bodies of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And they said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They still call Him “man,” these who had received such proof of His divinity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What do we?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They ought to have believed, and served, and bowed down to Him, and no longer to have deemed Him a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If we let him thus alone, the Romans will come, and will take away both our nation and city.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is it which they counsel to do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They wish to stir up the people, as though they themselves would be in danger on suspicion of establishing a kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if,” saith one of them, “the Romans learn that this Man is leading the multitudes, they will suspect us, and will come and destroy our city.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He teach revolt?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not permit you to give tribute to Cæsar?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did not ye wish to make Him a king, and He fly from you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not follow a mean and unpretending life, having neither house nor anything else of the kind?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They therefore said this, not from any such expectation, but from malice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet it so fell out contrary to their expectation, and the Romans took their nation and city when they had slain Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the things done by Him were beyond all suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He who healed the sick, and taught the most excellent way of life, and commanded men to obey their rulers, was not establishing but undoing a tyranny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “we conjecture from former (impostors).”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they taught revolt, He the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the words were but a pretense?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what action of the kind did He exhibit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He lead about with Him pompous guards? had He a train of chariots?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not seek the deserts?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they, that they may not seem to be speaking from their own ill feeling, say that all the city is in danger, that the common weal is being plotted against, and that they have to fear the worst.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These were not the causes of your captivity, but things contrary to them; both of this last, and of the Babylonish, and of that under Antiochus which followed: it was not that there were worshipers among you, but that there were among you those who did unjustly, and excited God to wrath, this caused you to be given up into bondage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But such a thing is envy, allowing men to see nothing which they ought to see, when it has once for all blinded the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not teach men to be meek?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not bid them when smitten on the right cheek to turn the other also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not bid them when injured to bear it? to show greater readi ness to endure evil, than others have to inflict it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Are these, tell me, the signs of one establishing a tyranny, and not rather of one pulling a tyranny down?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, as I said, a dreadful thing is malice, and full of hypocrisy; this hath filled the world with ten thousand evils; through this malady the law courts are filled, from this comes the desire of fame and wealth, from this the love of rule, and insolence, through this the roads have wicked robbers and the sea pirates, from this proceed the murders through the world, through this our race is rent asunder, and whatever evil thou mayest see, thou wilt perceive to arise from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This hath even burst into the churches, this hath caused ten thousand dreadful things from the beginning, this is the mother of avarice, this malady hath turned all things upside down, and corrupted justice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “gifts,” It saith, “blind the eyes of the wise, and as a muzzle on the mouth turn away reproofs.” ( Ecclus. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 , LXX. and marg. of E.V.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This makes slaves of freemen, concerning this we talk every day, and no good comes of it, we become worse than wild beasts; we plunder orphans, strip widows, do wrong to the poor, join woe to woe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Alas! that the righteous hath perished from the earth!” ( Mic. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is our part too henceforth to mourn, or rather we have need to say this every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We profit nothing by our prayers, nothing by our advice and exhortation, it remaineth therefore that we weep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus did Christ; after having many times exhorted those in Jerusalem, when they profited nothing, He wept at their hardness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also do the Prophets, and this let us do now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Henceforth is the season for mourning and tears and wailing; it is seasonable for us also to say now, “Call for the mourning women, and send for the cunning women, that they may cry aloud” ( Jer. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ); perhaps thus we shall be able to cast out the malady of those who build splendid houses, of those who surround themselves with lands gotten by rapine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is seasonable to mourn; but do ye take part with me in the mourning, ye who have been stripped and injured, by your mournings bring down my tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But while mourning we will mourn, not for ourselves but for them; they have not injured you, but they have destroyed themselves; for you have the Kingdom of heaven in return for the injustice done you, they hell in return for their gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account it is better to be injured than to injure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us bewail them with a lamentation not of man’s making, but that from the Holy Scriptures with which the Prophets also wailed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With Isaiah let us wail bitterly, and say, “Woe, they that add house to house, that lay field to field, that they may take somewhat from their neighbor; will ye dwell alone upon the earth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Great houses and fair, and there shall be no inhabitants in them.” ( Isa. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us mourn with Nahum, and say with him, “Woe to him that buildeth his house on high.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (Perhaps Jer. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather let us mourn for them as Christ mourned for those of old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Woe to you that are rich, for ye have received your consolation.” ( Luke vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us, I beseech you, not cease thus lamenting, and if it be not unseemly, let us even beat our breasts for the carelessness of our brethren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not weep for him who is already dead, but let us weep for the rapacious man, the grasping, the covetous, the insatiable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why should we mourn for the dead, in whose case it is impossible henceforth to effect anything?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us mourn for these who are capable even of change.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But while we are lamenting, perhaps they will laugh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even this is a worthy cause for lamentation, that they laugh when they ought to mourn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had they been at all affected by our sorrows, it would have behooved us to cease from sorrowing on account of their promise of amendment; but since they are of an insensible disposition, let us continue to weep, not merely for the rich, but for the lovers of money, the greedy, the rapacious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wealth is not an evil thing, (for we may use it rightly when we spend it upon those who have need,) but greediness is an evil, and it prepares deathless punishments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then bewail them; perhaps there will be some amendment; or even if they who have fallen in do not escape, others at least will not fall into the danger, but will guard against it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: May it come to pass that both they may be freed from their malady, and that none of us may ever fall into it, that we all may in common obtain the promised goods, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
